chapter_title.ar: حَدِيثُ أَبِي ذَرٍّ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على الشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 384)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على الشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (6 / 384)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ (عليه السلام) يَقُولُ يَنْبَغِي لِلْمُؤْمِنِ أَنْ يَخَافَ اللَّهَ تَبَارَكَ وَ تَعَالَى خَوْفاً كَأَنَّهُ مُشْرِفٌ عَلَى النَّارِ وَ يَرْجُوَهُ رَجَاءً كَأَنَّهُ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْجَنَّةِ ثُمَّ قَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عِنْدَ ظَنِّ عَبْدِهِ إِنْ خَيْراً فَخَيْراً وَ إِنْ شَرّاً فَشَرّاً.

en.hubeali: H 14910 – Yunus, from Sinan Bin Tareyf who said:

I heard Abu Abdullahasws saying: ‘It is befitting for ‘لِلْمُؤْمِنِ’ the Believer that he should fear Allahazwj with such a fear as if he is overlooking upon the Fire, and hope with such a hope as if he is from the inhabitants of the Paradise’. Then said: ‘Allahazwj is with the expectation of Hisazwj servant. If he expects good, so it will be good, and if he expects evil, so it will be evil’.
en.sarwar: 463. Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Sinan, from Ismail Bin Jabir who said: I was in the presence of Abu Abdullah (a.s.) at Makkah when a messenger from Al-Medina came to him (a.s.). So he (a.s.) said to him: ‘Who did you come with?’ He said, ‘I had no one to accompany me’. Abu Abdullah (a.s.) said to him: ‘But, if I (a.s.) had come to see you, I (a.s.) would have educated you of well manners’. Then said: ‘One is a Satan (la), and two are Satans (la), and three are a company, and four are friends’.
